ig_caption:
I use Notion AI like a second brain for my side hustle. Here's my system.

Most engineers use Notion as a fancy to-do list. That's the wrong move.

The knowledge problem engineers face:
→ Ideas scattered across Notes, Slack, emails, GitHub issues
→ Research done once — never found again
→ Side hustle decisions made from memory, not data
→ Start every new project from zero

My 4 core use cases:

1. Project Hub — one page per side project with goals, status, notes, revenue
2. Content Pipeline — ideas to published posts tracked in a database
3. Research Vault — AI summarizes articles, saves key points automatically
4. Docs on Demand — SOPs, emails, launch checklists drafted in 30 seconds

The Notion AI features I actually use:
→ Ask AI: query your entire workspace in plain English
→ Summarize: long notes → 5 bullet action items instantly
→ Write with AI: outlines, emails, product copy in seconds
→ Autofill DB: AI fills database columns from existing content

Time saved per week:
→ 2 hrs not hunting old notes
→ 1 hr not writing docs manually
→ 3 hrs total freed up = 156 hours/year

Your best ideas deserve a system, not a graveyard of sticky notes.

Notion is free to start. AI add-on is $10/mo and worth every dollar.

Follow for more tools that multiply your output.

ig_caption:
The engineer's path to $1M net worth. It's simpler than you think.

No crypto moonshots. No startup lottery. Just math and time.

Engineers have an unfair advantage:
→ Above-median income from day one
→ Analytical thinking = no emotional investing mistakes
→ Can build income-generating tools and products
→ Remote work = geo-arbitrage opportunities

The formula is boring. That's the point.

Income × Savings Rate → invested in index funds
+ Side hustle income → invested, not spent
+ Time → compound interest does the rest

$3,000/mo invested at 8% average return = $1M in about 16 years.

The 5-year roadmap:
Year 1–2: Max 401(k)/IRA. Emergency fund. Kill high-interest debt. Start side hustle.
Year 2–4: Side hustle income invested. Automate monthly index buys. Hit $100K net worth.
Year 4–5: Compound acceleration. $200K → $300K feels faster. Keep the boring system.

Mistakes that slow you down:
✗ Lifestyle inflation every raise
✗ Picking individual stocks or timing the market
✗ Spending side hustle income instead of investing it
✗ Waiting until you 'make more' to start

The compound math most people ignore:
$1K invested at 25 = ~$21K at 65 (7% return)
$1K invested at 35 = ~$10.7K at 65
2× difference from starting just 10 years earlier

$1M isn't a dream — it's a math problem you already know how to solve.

Your engineering brain optimized for problems is your biggest financial asset. Use it.

Follow for the engineer's path to financial freedom.

#WealthMindset #FinancialFreedom #EngineerLife #InvestingMindset #PassiveIncome #PersonalFinance #IndexFunds #CompoundInterest #SideHustle #BuildWealth

ig_caption:

AI designs the product. The platform handles printing and shipping. You collect the margin.

→ AI tools (Midjourney, DALL-E) replace a graphic designer
→ Niche engineering humor has a loyal, high-spending audience
→ Once listed, products earn while you work your day job

• Mugs & Posters — terminal output prints, circuit board art
• Stickers / Pins — low price, high volume, impulse buys

2. Midjourney / DALL-E 3 — generate 10 design variations in 20 min
3. Adobe Express (free) — upscale to 300dpi print-ready PNG
4. Printful + Etsy — first listing live in 30 min

Realistic numbers:

Your niche knowledge is your design moat. Non-engineers can't make engineering humor land.

ig_caption:
I post to Instagram and Threads every week. I haven't touched it in 3 months. Here's the full automation pipeline running this account.

The problem I was solving: I wanted to grow a social presence without the content treadmill. Engineer instinct — if I do a task more than 3 times, I automate it.

The full pipeline:

Step 1 — Content batch
Write carousel HTML → render PNGs with Playwright (1 weekend = 4-8 posts)

Step 2 — Push to GitHub
Images to repo assets/, captions to posts.json

Step 3 — GitHub Actions fires
Every Wednesday 11am ET — reads posts.json, posts to IG + Threads via API

The tech stack:
→ Design: HTML/CSS carousel + Playwright screenshot → 1080×1080 PNGs
→ Storage: GitHub repo + jsdelivr CDN (both free)
→ Automation: GitHub Actions cron (free, no server)
→ APIs: Instagram Graph API + Threads API (free)

Total monthly cost: $0. Everything runs on free tiers.

The cron job that runs it all:
cron: '53 14 * * 3' — Every Wednesday 14:53 UTC = 11am ET

What I actually spend time on:
→ Content strategy: ~1 hr/month
→ Writing carousel HTML: 30 min/post when batching
→ Reviewing logs: 5 min/week
→ Everything else: zero

Total active time: ~3 hours/month to maintain a weekly posting schedule.

The best content system is the one that runs without you showing up.

Follow to see the full repo walkthrough coming soon.

#BuildInPublic #ContentAutomation #GitHubActions #InstagramAPI #EngineerLife #SideHustle #AITools #NoCode #TechBTS #PassiveIncome

ig_caption:
GitHub Copilot vs Claude for engineers building a side hustle. Honest breakdown.

I've used both for 6+ months. Here's where each one actually wins.

What they're designed for:
→ Copilot: IDE-native code completion — thinks in lines, not systems
→ Claude: Long-context reasoning — thinks in files, architectures, tradeoffs
→ Copilot is faster for mechanical coding; Claude is better for decisions

For a side hustle, you spend more time deciding than typing.

Who wins by use case:
→ Boilerplate code: Copilot (faster inline, less thinking needed)
→ Architecture decisions: Claude (explains tradeoffs, not just code)
→ Writing landing copy: Claude (understands context and audience)
→ Debugging complex bugs: Claude (reasons through the whole file)
→ Content creation: Claude (Copilot can't touch this)

Pricing reality:
→ Copilot Individual: $10/mo — pure coding assistant
→ Claude Pro: $20/mo — coding + writing + strategy + research

For a side hustle, $20 on Claude replaces $10 Copilot + $20 ChatGPT + writing tools.

My actual setup:
→ Claude for planning, architecture, writing, debugging
→ Cursor AI (built on Claude) for in-editor coding
→ Dropped Copilot when Cursor came out

The right AI tool isn't the cheapest one. It's the one that ships your idea faster.

Pick based on what you're building, not what has the most GitHub stars.

Follow for more honest AI tool reviews for engineers.

#GitHubCopilot #ClaudeAI #AITools #EngineerLife #SideHustle #CursorAI #AIComparison #BuildInPublic #TechCareer #DeveloperTools

ig_caption:
The engineer's relationship with money is broken. Here's how to fix it.

We optimize systems at work. We leave our finances running on defaults.

The broken defaults most engineers run on:
✗ Income goes to checking account → gets spent → repeat
✗ 401(k) contribution set once in year one, never reviewed
✗ 'I'll invest when I earn more' — the perpetual delay loop
✗ Lifestyle inflates with every raise — net worth stays flat

An unoptimized financial system is a bug. Treat it like one.

The mindset shifts that change everything:
'I'll invest when I have more money' → 'I invest first, spend what's left'
'My salary determines my wealth' → 'My savings rate determines my wealth'
'That raise deserves a reward' → 'That raise = faster compounding'

The financial OS to build:
1. Automate: paycheck → 401(k) + Roth IRA + index funds before you can spend it
2. Track: net worth monthly, not spending daily (behavior follows measurement)
3. Add income: one side hustle that doesn't scale with your time
4. Raise the ceiling: salary negotiations, promotions, job changes

The 30-minute audit to run today:
→ 401(k) contribution — is it maxed? ($23,500 limit in 2025)
→ Fund allocation — still in target-date fund from year one?
→ Calculate savings rate (savings ÷ gross income × 100)
→ Set up one automatic transfer to a brokerage account

30 minutes today could be worth $100,000+ over 20 years. No other task has that ROI.

You optimize code for performance. Your money deserves the same treatment.

Follow for the engineer's 30-minute financial audit checklist.

#WealthMindset #EngineerFinance #PersonalFinance #InvestingForEngineers #401k #IndexFunds #FinancialFreedom #SideHustle #CompoundInterest #BuildWealth

ig_caption:
AI can write content that sounds exactly like you. Most people don't know how.

The secret isn't a better model. It's a better prompt.

Why AI content sounds generic:
✗ No voice instructions → AI defaults to corporate-neutral English
✗ Vague prompts → vague output ('write a LinkedIn post about AI')
✗ No audience definition → AI doesn't know who it's talking to
✗ No examples → AI can't pattern-match your style

The voice prompt framework:
'You are writing in the voice of [Name]. Audience: [specific description]. Tone: [2-3 adjectives]. Style: short sentences, no filler words, direct. Never say [banned phrases]. Always end with [CTA pattern]. Here are 3 examples of past content: [examples]'

Bad prompt: 'Write an Instagram post about using AI tools to make money as an engineer'

Good prompt: 'Write a 7-slide carousel for engineers 25–35 who want passive income. Direct, no fluff, one insight per slide. End with: Follow for more.'

5 elements of a voice prompt:
1. Identity — who are you? what's your credential?
2. Audience — age, job, pain points, what they care about
3. Tone — 2-3 specific adjectives (direct, technical, empowering)
4. Rules — what to avoid (jargon, corporate language, passive voice)
5. Examples — 2-3 samples of your best past content

My actual system prompt (simplified):
'You write for @alphaengineer.ai. Audience: engineers 25–40 building AI-powered passive income. Tone: direct, technical, motivating. No: harness, leverage, paradigm. Always: short sentences, real numbers, end with CTA.'

Every AI post I publish starts with this. The consistency is the brand.

AI doesn't replace your voice. It amplifies it — if you give it the right instructions.

Follow — I'll share my full prompt template next week.

#AITools #ContentCreation #AIWriting #ClaudeAI #PromptEngineering #EngineerLife #SideHustle #BuildInPublic #ContentStrategy #PersonalBrand
threads_text:

ig_caption:
1 year building this account in public. What I got wrong. What actually worked.

No vanity metrics. Just honest lessons from the process.

What I got wrong at the start:
✗ Spent 3 weeks 'perfecting' the first post instead of shipping it
✗ Tried to be everywhere at once (IG, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube)
✗ Measured success by follower count instead of content consistency
✗ Waited for 'the right moment' to monetize — there is no right moment

The 4 lessons that actually mattered:
1. Consistency beats quality at the start. Post something imperfect every week.
2. The system matters more than the content. Automate before you scale.
3. Niche specificity compounds. 'Engineer + AI + passive income' beats 'productivity.'
4. The audience you want finds you when you stop trying to impress the wrong one.

What the pipeline looks like now:
→ 1 weekend/month: batch 4-8 carousel posts (HTML → Playwright → PNG)
→ GitHub Actions posts every Wednesday 11am ET automatically
→ IG + Threads in sync — two platforms, one post, zero extra work
→ 3 hours/month of active work. Consistent output regardless of schedule.

What I'd do differently from day 1:
→ Build the automation pipeline before post #1, not after post #20
→ Start collecting emails from the first post (the list is the asset)
→ Ship imperfect content weekly instead of perfect content quarterly
→ Say no to platforms until one is fully automated

The honest numbers:
→ 40+ carousels created
→ 2 platforms posting automatically
→ $0 monthly infrastructure cost
→ Revenue: building. The foundation takes time. The compound curve hits later.

Building in public means sharing the real process, not just the highlight reel.

Follow for the full unfiltered build journal.

#BuildInPublic #BehindTheScenes #EngineerLife #ContentAutomation #SideHustle #GitHubActions #AITools #PassiveIncome #PersonalBrand #TechCreator

ig_caption:
Why engineers are bad at salary negotiation — and how to fix it. 💰

The 4 mental blocks costing you $20K–$50K per year.

The broken defaults:
✗ "My work speaks for itself" — it doesn't. Advocacy speaks for you.
✗ "Negotiating is rude" — it's expected. Companies budget for it.
✗ "I'm lucky to have this offer" — scarcity mindset, not leverage mindset.
✗ "I don't know my worth" — you can find out in 20 minutes.

The 3-step script:
1. Never give a number first. "What's the budgeted range?"
2. Anchor 15–20% above your target. They'll meet in the middle.
3. Use silence. After your ask, stop talking. Silence is leverage.

Know your market rate in 20 min:
→ Levels.fyi — real comp data from verified engineers
→ LinkedIn Salary — filter by role, location, experience
→ Blind — anonymous, see what peers are actually paid

The lifetime cost of not negotiating:
→ $15K typical first win → $300K compounded over 20 years → captured in 10 minutes

You negotiate for systems at work every day. Do it for yourself.

#SalaryNegotiation #WealthMindset #EngineerLife #FinancialFreedom #TechCareer #PersonalFinance #CareerGrowth #BuildWealth #Money #Engineering

Engineers are bad at salary negotiation — and it costs $20K–$50K a year. 💰

ig_caption:
Google NotebookLM: the AI research tool most engineers are sleeping on. 📚

Free. Powerful. Understands your documents better than you do.

What makes it different:
→ Upload YOUR documents — PDFs, research papers, meeting notes
→ NotebookLM reads them and becomes an expert on your specific material
→ Every answer cites the exact source (page number)
→ No hallucinations on your content — grounded in what you uploaded

My 5 use cases as an engineer:
📄 Technical docs — upload 200-page manuals, ask in plain English
📚 Research papers — synthesize 10 papers into one report with citations
🎧 Audio overview — turn any document into a podcast episode
→ Upload competitor landing pages — ask "what's their positioning?"
→ Upload 6 months of meeting notes — find patterns and action items

Get started in 5 minutes:
1. notebooklm.google.com (free with Google account)
2. Upload 1–5 PDFs or paste URLs
3. Ask "What are the main themes across all these sources?"

ig_caption:
Zapier AI vs Make.com — which automation tool is actually better? ⚙️

I've built side-hustle pipelines with both. Honest verdict.

Zapier AI:
→ 7,000+ app integrations
→ AI-guided setup in plain English
→ Easier to learn, faster to start
→ $20–$49/mo for real usage

Make.com:
→ Visual scenario builder (engineers will love this)
→ Complex logic — filters, routers, iterators, aggregators
→ Much lower cost per operation at scale
→ $9/mo handles high-volume automations

Use Zapier when:
✓ You need a specific integration only Zapier supports
✓ Low-volume automations (under 2,000 tasks/month)
✓ Speed matters more than cost

Use Make.com when:
✓ Complex logic (routing by brand, content type, platform)
✓ High-volume automations (10,000+ operations/month)
✓ Budget matters — 5–10x cheaper at scale

My personal setup:
→ Make.com for social content pipelines (Claude → Webhook → Buffer → IG/FB)
→ 2 brands × 30 posts/month with custom routing for $9/mo
→ Zapier for one-off integrations Make.com doesn't support

The best automation tool is the one you'll actually use and maintain.

ig_caption:
5 AI prompts engineers use every day to save 3 hours. ⏱️

Copy these. Use them today. They work immediately.

Prompt #1 — Debug (saves 45 min):
"You are a senior [language] engineer. Here is my code: [paste]. It's failing with: [error]. Diagnose the root cause. Don't fix it yet — just explain what's wrong."

Prompt #2 — Email draft (saves 20 min):
"Write a [formal/casual] email to [role]. Subject: [topic]. Key points: [list]. Max 200 words. No filler phrases."

Prompt #3 — Code review (saves 30 min):
"Review for: 1) Security vulnerabilities 2) Performance issues 3) Missing edge cases. Be specific — name line numbers."

Prompt #4 — Auto-document (saves 40 min):
"Write docstrings for every function in this file. Follow [Google/NumPy/JSDoc] format. Include: what it does, params, return, one example."

Prompt #5 — Market research (saves 45 min):
"Write a 200-word brief on [topic]. Include: current state, 3 key trends in 2025, and 2 risks engineers should know. Cite reasoning."

The compound effect: 3h/day × 250 work days = 750 hours/year reclaimed.

Save these. Use them this week.

#ClaudeAI #AITools #EngineerLife #Productivity #AIPrompts #SideHustle #BuildInPublic #TechCareer #AIWorkflow #TimeHacking

ig_caption:
Why most engineers never reach financial freedom — and how the top 10% think differently. 🧠

The myth: "If I just earn more, I'll eventually be free."
The reality: lifestyle grows with every raise. Same savings rate.

The savings rate math most engineers ignore:
→ 10% savings rate → Financial independence in ~43 years
→ 20% savings rate → ~37 years
→ 40% savings rate → ~22 years
→ 60% savings rate → ~12 years
→ 75% savings rate → ~7 years

Same income. Completely different timelines.

4 mindset shifts from the top 10%:
→ "I'll invest more when I earn more" → "I invest first, live on what's left"
→ "That raise = better lifestyle" → "That raise = 5 years shorter to freedom"
→ "My salary determines my wealth" → "My savings rate determines my wealth"
→ "Wait until I make more" → "Run the system now"

The 30-min audit to run today:
→ Calculate your savings rate: monthly investments ÷ gross income × 100
→ Check 401(k) contribution — maxed or just employer match minimum?
→ List subscriptions — what auto-renews that you forgot about?
→ Calculate FI number: annual expenses × 25

The difference isn't income. It's who controls the system.

#WealthMindset #FinancialFreedom #EngineerFinance #SavingsRate #FIRE #PersonalFinance #CompoundInterest #InvestingMindset #BuildWealth #TechCareer

ig_caption:
I use GitHub Copilot and Claude together. They're not competing — they're complementary. 🤝

Here's the exact workflow, and when to use which.

The core difference most engineers miss:
→ Copilot: IDE-native autocomplete. Thinks in lines and functions. Sees your current file.
→ Claude: Long-context reasoning. Thinks in architecture and tradeoffs. Sees your whole problem.

Copilot wins:
✅ Boilerplate and repetitive patterns
✅ Test stubs
✅ Fast autocomplete mid-flow when context is obvious

Claude wins:
✅ Architecture decisions
✅ Debugging complex bugs
✅ Code review and tradeoff analysis
✅ Writing copy, docs, and email

My actual workflow:
1. Open Claude: "Design the architecture for [feature]. What are the tradeoffs?"
2. Claude gives a structured recommendation. I pick a direction.
3. Switch to editor (Cursor with Copilot). Copilot autocompletes the patterns.
4. Hit a bug? Back to Claude with the full error context.

Claude handles the WHY. Copilot handles the WHAT.

The pricing reality:
→ Copilot Individual: $10/mo
→ Claude Pro: $20/mo
→ Both: $30/mo
→ What they replace: $99/mo in separate tools

$30 all-in is one of the best ROIs in any engineer's stack.

ig_caption:
Stop checking your bank account. Check your net worth instead. 📊

One number tells the whole story. The other tells you almost nothing.

Bank balance = cash you have right now. It resets every month. Doesn't show your investments, your debt, or your real financial trajectory.

Net worth = all assets − all liabilities. It compounds. It shows whether you're actually getting wealthier or just earning-and-spending at a higher level.

Same salary, two engineers. One has $150K net worth at 35. The other has $15K. The bank balance doesn't tell you which you are.

The 10-minute setup — what to track:

Assets (add):
→ Liquid: checking, savings, emergency fund
→ Investments: 401k, Roth IRA, brokerage, ETFs

Liabilities (subtract):
→ Student loans, car loan, credit card balance

= Net Worth. Calculate this once. Update monthly.

The monthly ritual that works:
1. First of every month: log current balances
2. Total assets − total liabilities
3. Track month-over-month change
4. Ask: "What moved the number? What can I improve?"

15 minutes. More financial clarity than daily bank balance checking.

The motivation effect:
→ Checking bank balance: anxiety
→ Checking net worth: progress
→ You can't control the balance directly. You control the net worth.

Tool that makes it easy (free): Personal Capital / Empower — automatic, syncs all accounts.

Behavior follows measurement. Measure the right thing.

Calculate yours today. Comment the month-over-month change you'd like to hit by year end 👇

#WealthMindset #NetWorth #FinancialFreedom #PersonalFinance #EngineerLife #Investing #BuildWealth #SideHustle #FIRE #FinancialIndependence #CompoundInterest

→ Start collecting emails from your first post.
→ Post imperfect content weekly. Not perfect content quarterly.
→ Pick one platform and automate it fully before adding a second.

→ 2 platforms auto-posting weekly
→ $20/month total cost
→ Revenue: building. Email list: growing.

Building in public means showing the real process — not just the wins.

Follow for the full automation build journal. See you next Wednesday.

#BuildInPublic #BehindTheScenes #ContentAutomation #EngineerLife #SideHustle #InstagramAutomation #AITools #GrowthHacking #SoloFounder #FinancialFreedom #PassiveIncome
threads_text:
6 months of automated content. The honest data, not the highlight reel. 📈

What performed best:
• Specific numbers — "saved 3 hours" beats "saves time"
• Real story posts beat generic comparisons
• "Do THIS today" beats "here's something interesting"

What flopped:
• Generic AI listicles, vague CTAs, and no hook in the first line

The setup: 60+ carousels, 2 platforms auto-posting weekly, $20/mo, ~3 hours of active work a month.

Which matters more to you — the system or the content?
